core_principle.statement: Visual language is a structured engineering communication system, not an aesthetic style.
core_principle.interpretation: Every visual element must represent architecture, engineering logic, or system behavior.

visual_identity_foundation.visual_goal: Make engineering systems visible through structured visual rules

core_visual_pillars.name: Engineering Structure
core_visual_pillars.definition: Represents logic, precision, and system stability

core_visual_pillars.name: Architectural Space
core_visual_pillars.definition: Represents spatial thinking and environmental clarity

core_visual_pillars.name: System Transformation
core_visual_pillars.definition: Represents conversion from design to execution

geometry_rules.grid_dependency: All visual elements must align to 10x10 engineering grid system

composition_principles.hierarchy: Clear vertical and horizontal system hierarchy must always exist
composition_principles.alignment: All elements must be grid-aligned without exception
composition_principles.balance: Engineering layer defines structure, architectural layer defines space
composition_principles.focus: Visual attention must always move toward system logic, not decoration

visual_flow_logic.reading_direction: Left-to-right or top-to-bottom depending on layout context
visual_flow_logic.focus_mechanism: Attention guided toward transformation point (gold node)

typography_integration.rule: Typography must behave as part of visual system, not separate layer
typography_integration.alignment: Must follow grid and structural logic

strategic_goal.objective: Create a unified visual system that behaves like an engineering architecture model
strategic_goal.outcome: Instant recognition of AvizSazeh as a structured architectural engineering system brand
